summary: My name is Beatriz Ibarra de Botero. I was born in Colombia in 1948 and have lived through many changes, including moving to the United States to join my children. I have been married for 46 years to my husband, and we have five children. Since arriving here over five years ago, I have been working and studying English, hoping to apply for citizenship soon. I love life, nature, and am grateful for the peaceful life we have here.

I was born in Colombia, specifically in Garzón, Huila, and later lived in Bogotá and Cali. Colombia is where my mother got sick and where I had to care for her until she passed away from cancer. Life there was different, especially in terms of traffic and culture, which I find less orderly compared to here. Despite the challenges, Colombia is my homeland and the place where my family roots are strong.

Garzón, Huila is the city in Colombia where I was born in 1948. It was my first home before moving to Bogotá with my family. Later, when my mother became ill with cancer, she returned to Huila, and I was asked to come back to care for her. This place holds important memories of my early life and family struggles.

After being born in Garzón, Huila, my family moved to Bogotá where I spent part of my childhood. However, when my mother fell ill, she moved to Cali, and I was left to care for my younger siblings. Bogotá was an important place in my early life before the family changes took us to other cities.

Cali became my home after my mother moved there due to illness. I worked there and eventually met the father of my children. We fell in love, married, and had five children while living in Cali. It was a significant place for me as a young adult and mother before my children began moving abroad.

I came to the United States to join several of my children who had already moved here. We have been living here for over five years now, and I am working and studying English to apply for citizenship. The U.S. has offered me a peaceful life with respectful people and good transportation, especially here in Minnesota where I chose to settle.

One of my younger daughters is currently living in Canada pursuing a PhD. My family has spread across countries, with some children in the U.S. and one in Canada. We have discussed where we would like to spend our later years, and although Canada was an option, I preferred to stay in Minnesota.

Minnesota is where I currently live and where I chose to settle after my daughter asked where we wanted to be in our later years. I love the climate, the seasons, and the kindness of the people here. The respect for pedestrians and the good transportation system make life here very comfortable compared to Colombia.

Another one of my daughters lives in Dallas, Texas, and we considered moving there for our later years. However, after thinking about it, I decided I preferred Minnesota because of its environment and community. Dallas is part of our family’s spread across the U.S., but it was not my first choice to settle down.

Texas is where my daughter in Dallas lives, and it was one of the options for where my husband and I might spend our final years. Although Texas was considered, I felt more comfortable staying in Minnesota because of the lifestyle and environment. Texas remains important to us because of family connections.
